name: Pumble Team Communication API
what_it_does: Free team messaging and communication platform with webhook and bot API — enabling teams to communicate via channels, direct messages, and threads with unlimited message history (free), and basic API support for incoming webhooks and app integrations, targeting budget-conscious teams as a Slack alternative.
use_cases: Sending channel notifications via Pumble Incoming Webhook for CI/CD and alert notifications
Building bot integrations via Pumble Bot API for team workflow automation
Team communication for budget-constrained organizations using free unlimited message history
Migrating from Slack via Pumble for cost reduction in SMB team communication
Receiving external notifications in Pumble channels via webhook for monitoring integrations
not_for: Enterprise with complex compliance and security requirements (use Slack Enterprise or Teams)
Teams needing rich app marketplace with 2,000+ integrations (Slack far ahead in ecosystem)
Organizations requiring advanced enterprise features (SSO, SCIM, E2EE)
best_when: A small team or startup needs free team messaging with unlimited message history — where Pumble provides Slack-like functionality without the cost, with basic webhook API for notifications.
avoid_when: You need enterprise security features, extensive app integrations, or advanced compliance.

auth.notes: Incoming webhooks: unique webhook URL (no auth — URL is the secret). Bot API with API token. No full REST API for data retrieval. Limited to posting messages via webhook and responding to slash commands via bots. No OAuth2 app marketplace. API capabilities significantly more limited than Slack.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: unlimited message history, unlimited users, unlimited channels
pricing.notes: Serbian company (Belgrade). Part of CAKE.com (also makes Clockify and Vacation Tracker). Founded 2021. Key differentiator: unlimited free message history (Slack limits to 90 days on free). 50,000+ teams. Very fast growth due to aggressive free tier. Bot API for slash commands. Incoming webhooks for notifications. Limited API compared to Slack. Used by: small businesses, nonprofits, startups. Video calls built-in (limited). iOS/Android apps. German, Portuguese, French localizations. Note: API capabilities are basic — primarily webhooks, not full programmatic access.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Webhooks only (no full REST API). Incoming webhook URL as auth. Bot API for slash commands. GDPR. Serbian company (EU). Free unlimited messages. Very limited API compared to Slack. Not suitable for agents needing read access to messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Message posting via webhook: not idempotent — duplicates possible. Channel/workspace reads: limited API access. Implement application-level deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: No full REST API — only incoming webhooks (post messages) and bot slash commands
Cannot READ messages via API — only post/receive; unsuitable for message retrieval automation
Incoming webhook URL is the auth credential — treat as secret
Serbian company (EU-adjacent) — GDPR compliant but not EU-headquartered
API is minimal compared to Slack/Teams — limited integration possibilities
Primary value is free unlimited messages — not API capability

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. Serbian company. Webhook URL as auth (must protect). No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. EU data residency. Limited API surface reduces attack surface. Team chat data is sensitive internal communication. Young platform (2021) — limited security audit history.
